finding: A sealed runner `cache-hit-skip` build still writes timing/buildDecision metadata into tracked release manifests, making release/atm-onefile/release-manifest.json and release/atm-root-drop/release-manifest.json dirty even though no build output changed.
expectedBehavior: A no-op cache-hit build should not mutate tracked release artifacts. Cache-hit evidence should live in the runner-sync receipt or runtime telemetry unless the artifact content genuinely changed.
evidenceOrRepro: On 2026-07-19, rerunning `npm run build` after runner-sync enqueue produced `[sealed-runner-build] cache-hit-skip full` with receipt totalElapsed 770ms, but `git diff` showed only release manifest metadata changes from cache-miss timings to cache-hit timings.
followUp: Fold into or cross-link ATM-GOV-0191: separate no-op cacheHitSkip receipts from tracked release artifact metadata and ensure cache-hit paths leave the worktree clean.
